title: Biology Has Evolved. Regulation Hasn’t. Here’s the Fallout
summary: SynBioBeta interviews three biotech regulation experts about the fragmented patchwork of U.S. regulatory agencies overseeing synthetic biology products, primarily the FDA, EPA, and USDA-APHIS operating under the 1986 Coordinated Framework. The article details how outdated regulations create regulatory hurdles that disproportionately burden smaller startups while benefiting larger corporations with more resources, and discusses potential reforms including emerging FDA pathways for accelerated approval of gene therapies for rare conditions.

entities.name: National Security Commission on Emerging Biotechnology
